text: Evaluate tokenmasterx submodule for ideas and features to integrate into autoharness
Add graphtor as a capability pack in the full suite
Run session with read access to all capability pack workspaces; coordinate standardized logging and telemetry to enable full measurability
When engram capability pack is installed, need a deterministic hook mechanism to enforce engram usage in the workspace

graphtor-docs needs a similar hook mechanism in domain-specific context environments, which may include tech documentation but also business context information such as SoWs, company-specific internal documentation, business process information, and data mapping documentation

Evaluate shifting harness metrics, reporting, and evals out of backlogit; also consider a data contract for tool telemetry to standardize how autoharness measures tool usage and token efficiency through tooling
Expand harness to be multi-repo capable where a workspace spans multiple codebases or is a monorepo

Remove ping-loop.prompt for agent-intercom: MCP mode is being removed entirely from agent-intercom, consolidating strictly on ACP mode. (Conflicts with 067.001-T in shipment 079-S which installs ping-loop.prompt.)

Consider how to enable Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL) on the dev box to perform local Linux-based builds, tests, lint, and format that mirror the GitHub Actions Linux runner (ubuntu-latest), so that Linux verification is done LOCALLY rather than on GitHub Actions. Goal: offload GitHub Actions and minimize CI gate costs by shifting Linux builds off GHA onto the local devbox. Ties directly into the 069-F unified CI + local-gating primitive and the cross-platform pre-push hook (.githooks/pre-push): extend local gating so it can run the Linux build/test inside WSL, then reduce or skip redundant remote Linux CI (the same rationale graphtor uses for Linux-only regular CI: Windows is covered by local dev builds, so re-running on GHA is redundant). The harness capability (install + tune): detect WSL availability and a suitable distro/toolchain, provide a WSL-backed local Linux build/test runner, wire it into the pre-push hook and/or the local-gating primitive, and document how it reduces CI reliance. Considerations to work through: WSL detection and setup, distro/toolchain provisioning, Windows<->WSL path translation, reproducibility/parity with the GHA runner image, and recording local Linux-build evidence that satisfies the PR readiness full-local-build requirement (so the ci gate / merge readiness can trust local WSL verification and the remote workflow can be gated to run only when local evidence is absent). Likely starts with a spike to prove feasibility and parity before wiring it into the primitive.
